ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for blended iced white chocolate mocha coffee beverages; 'coffee beverage' (FOODON_03301589) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FHR covers coffee-based beverages broadly; no specific code exists for blended iced Frappuccino-style drinks. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for blended iced coffee beverage with white chocolate flavouring; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard tall (12 fl oz / 354 ml) serving of White Chocolate Mocha Frappuccino weighs approximately 370 g and delivers roughly 155 kcal, 38 g carbohydrate, and 37 g sugar.

nutrient_source.title: White Chocolate Mocha Frappuccino — Singapore HPB Foodo nutrient database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values per 100 g taken directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for White Chocolate Mocha Frappuccino (cr_id G00010293). Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several micronutrients are not reported in the HPB entry and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — sugar-sweetened milk-based beverages analogous estimate; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for White Chocolate Mocha Frappuccino in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 62 (medium) based on the closest analogous entry: sweetened flavoured milk-based beverages with added sugar (GI ~60–65) from Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (62) × carbohydrate per serving (38 g) / 100 = 23.6, rounded to 23.5.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (9.94 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently elevates blood pressure risk; sodium is low at 3 mg/100 g
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this beverage because its high free-sugar load (9.94 g/100 g; ~37 g per tall serving) independently raises blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidance on sugar-sweetened beverages, even though sodium content is low.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened blended beverage with 9.94 g sugar/100 g, zero dietary fibre, and medium GI (~62); liquid sugar raises postprandial glucose rapidly
type2_diabetes.guidance: People managing type 2 diabetes should avoid this beverage: its 9.94 g sugar/100 g (approximately 37 g per tall serving), zero fibre, and medium GI (~62) combine to produce a high glycemic load (~23.5 per serving), causing rapid postprandial glucose excursions; a sugar-free syrup version with no whipped cream is the only clinically acceptable substitute.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free-sugar content from white chocolate sauce contributes to hypertriglyceridaemia and excess caloric intake; saturated fat from milk and white chocolate sauce is an additional concern
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, regular consumption of this high-sugar blended coffee beverage (9.94 g sugar/100 g; ~37 g per serving) is inadvisable as excess free-sugar intake raises triglycerides and contributes to weight gain, both established CVD risk factors; the white chocolate sauce and low-fat milk also contribute saturated fat that should be accounted for within daily limits.

llm_training.natural_language_description: White Chocolate Mocha Frappuccino is a blended iced coffee beverage sold by Starbucks, made from Frappuccino roast coffee, white chocolate flavoured sauce, low-fat milk, and ice. The drink is served cold without whipped cream in the Singapore HPB-recorded formulation. It has a sweet, creamy, milky flavour with a mild coffee undertone and a smooth, slushy texture from blending with ice. Per 100 g, it provides 42 kcal, 10.36 g carbohydrate (of which 9.94 g is sugar), 0.25 g fat, and negligible protein (0 g by HPB formulation). A standard tall serving (~370 g) delivers approximately 155 kcal and 37 g sugar. The beverage is ultra-processed, containing white chocolate syrup as a primary flavour contributor. Clinically, it is a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) and is unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its high free-sugar content, high glycemic load (~23.5 per tall serving), and zero dietary fibre. It is vegetarian, halal-friendly, and contains milk as a declared allergen. It is widely available in Singapore Starbucks outlets and is popular as an all-day treat beverage.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a White Chocolate Mocha Frappuccino?
Is White Chocolate Mocha Frappuccino suitable for diabetics?
How much sugar is in a Starbucks White Mocha Frappuccino?
Can I drink White Chocolate Frappuccino if I have high blood pressure?
What is the GI of a Frappuccino?
Is White Chocolate Mocha Frappuccino halal?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values per 100 g are taken directly from the HPB Foodo database (cr_id G00010293, confidence 0.95), which is an approved primary regional source for Singapore food records. The match is exact. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables, which is the approved GI source.
notes: protein_g is 0.0 per the HPB Foodo database entry; this is a confirmed zero by the approved source for this ultra-processed blended beverage formulation — the white chocolate sauce and low-fat milk contribute negligible protein at the diluted per-100 g level as reported. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 confirmed per HPB — this is a liquid beverage with no fibre-containing whole-food ingredients. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 confirmed per HPB. magnesium_mg is 0.0 confirmed per HPB. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this product. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this product as of 2026-04-16. GI of 62 is an analogous estimate based on sweetened flavoured milk-based beverages from Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. Serving size of 370 g corresponds to a Starbucks tall (12 fl oz / 354 ml) cup, density approximated at ~1.045 g/ml for a blended iced milk-based beverage. The input record description notes drinks are served without whipped cream; nutrient values reflect this formulation.
